intro: Macropeplus schwackeanus is a plant species belonging to the family Celastraceae, commonly known as the staff vine. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Macropeplus schwackeanus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Macropeplus schwackeanus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues and reduce inflammation.
